item.title: Hearing of the Committee on Economic and Monetary Affairs of the European Parliament
item.speaker: Christine Lagarde

rating.summary: President Lagarde testified before the European Parliament's ECON Committee, reviewing the euro area's economic outlook amid a new Middle East conflict that has pushed inflation to 3.2% and weighed on growth. The ECB raised rates by 25 basis points at its June 2026 meeting, framing the move as a measured, graduated response to a supply shock that is too large to look through but does not yet show signs of de-anchoring expectations. Lagarde emphasized a data-dependent, meeting-by-meeting approach with no pre-commitment to a particular rate path.
rating.stance_rationale: The speech is modestly hawkish: the ECB has just hiked 25bp and frames further action as possible if the shock intensifies, but Lagarde stresses gradualism and notes no evidence of second-round effects requiring a more forceful response, keeping the overall tone balanced-to-mildly hawkish.
key_quotes: The shock is too large to look through without jeopardising our target. But we see no evidence yet of de-anchoring of inflation expectations or second-round effects that would warrant a more forceful policy response at this stage.
We are not pre-committing to a particular rate path.
